summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by reading from Mark 2, the story of Jesus healing a paralyzed man lowered through a roof by his four friends. He used this story to reflect on the explosive growth of Impact Church, noting how the crowd's declaration, 'We have never seen anything like this,' mirrors his own experience of God's work in their community. The sermon's core message was a call to active, compassionate faith. Pastor Travis emphasized that true Christ-followers are marked by three qualities demonstrated by the four friends: compassion, commitment, and courage. Compassion involves moving beyond sympathy to empathetic action, feeling the burdens of others as Jesus did. Commitment means persevering through the 'crowds'—the obstacles of life—to bring people to Jesus. Courage is the faith to take bold, unconventional steps so that others can encounter Christ. He challenged the congregation to use their influence as 'salt and light,' inviting others to church and living in a way that clearly points to God. The message concluded with an invitation for personal salvation and a call to prepare for the upcoming service, encouraging everyone to bring someone new to experience God's work at Impact Church.
mainPoints: Followers of Christ are called to demonstrate deep compassion, moving beyond sympathy to empathetic action that bears the burdens of others.
Genuine commitment to Christ and others requires perseverance through obstacles, refusing to let the 'crowds' of life block access to Jesus.
Living out our faith requires courage to take bold, unconventional steps so that spiritually paralyzed people can encounter the healing power of Christ.

versesMentioned.text: A few days later, when Jesus again entered Capernaum, the people heard that he had come home. They gathered in such large numbers that there was no room left, not even outside the door, and he preached the word to them. Some men came, bringing to him a paralyzed man, carried by four of them. Since they could not get him to Jesus because of the crowd, they made an opening in the roof above Jesus by digging through it and then lowered the mat the man was lying on. When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' ... He got up, took his mat and walked out in full view of them all. This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, 'We have never seen anything like this!'

You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled underfoot. You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden. Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.

Therefore go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to obey everything I have commanded you. And surely I am with you always, to the very end of the age.
He said to them, 'Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.'
But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

Monday.scripture: Mark 2:5 - 'When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralyzed man, "Son, your sins are forgiven."'
Monday.action: Identify one person in your life who needs spiritual encouragement. Commit to praying for them today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me eyes to see the needs of those around me and faith to believe you can meet them.

Tuesday.action: Practice empathy. In a conversation today, seek to understand the other person's perspective before offering advice.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, soften my heart. Help me to feel with others as Jesus did, not just feel for them.

Wednesday.scripture: Matthew 5:13 - 'You are the salt of the earth.'
Wednesday.action: Intentionally bring flavor and light to one environment today (home, work, online) through a kind word or positive post.
Wednesday.prayer: God, help me to season my world with your grace today. Keep me from blending into the darkness.

Thursday.scripture: Matthew 5:14 - 'You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden.'
Thursday.action: Do one 'good deed' today with the simple motive of glorifying God, not yourself.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, let my actions today point others to you, the source of all light and goodness.

Friday.scripture: Acts 1:8 - 'You will be my witnesses... to the ends of the earth.'
Friday.action: Take a step of courage. Invite someone to church or share a simple reason for your hope in Christ.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, give me boldness and the right words to be a witness for you today.

Saturday.scripture: Mark 2:12 - 'This amazed everyone and they praised God, saying, "We have never seen anything like this!"'
Saturday.action: Prepare for worship tomorrow. Pray for the services and for those who will be invited, expecting God to do amazing things.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, I come expecting to see your power at work. Do something in our church that causes people to praise you in awe.
